assertions: buildDnsRecordSet returns exactly 6 records: 3 DKIM CNAME, SPF TXT, MAIL FROM MX+SPF, DMARC TXT
MAIL FROM MX feedback-smtp.<region>.amazonses.com prio 10 + SPF TXT
calls injected SES createDomainIdentity (Easy DKIM)+MAIL FROM (mocked), no real SES
from_domain from input not client workspace_id
DKIM gate from SES status NOT DNS: SES Success->dkim_verified true even if DNS pending; SES Pending->false even if all DNS found
allowed=false when SES DKIM not verified even if all DNS found (SES is gate)
allowed=false when DKIM verified but required DNS record pending/mismatch
allowed=true only when SES verified AND required records resolve
gate allows -> createConfigurationSet once with workspace config-set name + shared IP pool (aws-sdk-client-mock)
gate denies -> no createConfigurationSet, no status flip
throws on invalid MJML (never save uncompilable)
List-Unsubscribe wraps URL (+optional mailto) in angle brackets
buildTemplateUpsert INSERT email_templates with mjml AND compiled_html
workspace_id bound values[0], required, throws on empty
both raw mjml + compiled_html in values
real PG, skipIf no DB, unique namespace + cleanup
after start-domain persisted sending_identity has from_domain/dkim_tokens/record_checks pending/verified:false/ip_mode:shared, status still onboarding canSend false
SES DKIM Pending -> activate rejected, status stays onboarding, no config_set, createConfigurationSet not called
SES DKIM Success + records resolved -> activate via runPlanInWorkspaceTx flips status=active, verified=true, config_set set; canSend true
activate UPDATE binds workspace_id $1, touches only target
real PG, two workspaces A+B status=onboarding same from_domain
B untouched: status onboarding, verified false, no config_set
A record_checks don't appear on B
activate UPDATE rowCount=1 and row is A
real PG, save via buildTemplateUpsert -> email_templates row with workspace_id,name,mjml,non-empty compiled_html
template under A not visible scoped to B
real PG, check-domain with mocked DNS+SES persists record_checks
SES Success+records updates dkim signal but does NOT flip status (activate's job)

mocks: SES wrapper via aws-sdk-client-mock (@aws-sdk/client-sesv2): createDomainIdentity Easy DKIM, getIdentity DKIM-attrs reader, createConfigurationSet — injectable, assert args (shared pool, config-set name), never real SES
Injected DNS resolver (TXT/CNAME/MX) canned answers, never real DNS
Injected SES verification-status reader (DKIM Pending/Success) decoupled from DNS so SES-is-the-gate is provable
mjml: real (not mocked) in compile tests
implUnits: packages/email: injectable SesEmailClient interface + prod impl over @aws-sdk/client-sesv2 (+aws-sdk-client-mock dev)
no DDL: sending_identity jsonb already exists; document new keys
order: email builders+compile+SES iface -> onboarding cores -> entrypoints+deps -> template save -> integration
notes: Highest-value: (a) canSend false until status=active && verified=true; (b) SES status (not DNS, not registrar) is the activate gate, proven by decoupled mocks (DNS-all-found+SES-pending denies; SES-success+DNS-required-pending denies); (c) DMARC recommended not gate-required; (d) workspace-scoping: activate UPDATE binds workspace_id $1, touches exactly one row. SES SDK is @aws-sdk/client-sesv2 (CreateEmailIdentity Easy DKIM, PutEmailIdentityMailFromAttributes, GetEmailIdentity, CreateConfigurationSet) — confirm exact command names when adding dep. mjml real in compile tests. sending_identity jsonb already in §6 schema, no DDL. Reuse runPlanInWorkspaceTx/scopedQuery/adminPool/hasDatabaseUrl + unique namespace + cleanup.
